metadata.description: Iterative cycles where agent outputs inform subsequent actions, enabling continuous improvement and adaptation.
Ability of agents to detect and fix their own errors during or after task execution.
Agents that autonomously improve their capabilities over time through experience and self-modification.
Coordination and management of multiple AI agents working together on complex tasks.
Ability of LLM agents to learn, select, and utilize external tools and APIs to extend their capabilities.
Mechanisms for agents to store, retrieve, and manage information across interactions and sessions.
Ensuring AI agents operate within safe boundaries and do not cause unintended harm.
Generating well-formed, schema-compliant outputs from LLMs for reliable downstream processing.
Identifying critical decision points in agent trajectories where errors originate or diverge.
An authoritative mechanism for verifying the correctness of agent actions and outputs.
Techniques for identifying the precise location and cause of errors in agent execution traces.
Prompting technique where models generate intermediate reasoning steps before final answers.
Learning paradigm where agents improve through reward signals from environment interactions.
Search algorithm that uses random sampling to explore decision trees for optimal action selection.
Using compiler-based checks to validate generated code and structured outputs.
Using adversarial agents to critique and improve other agents' outputs.
Anchoring agent outputs to verified source material to reduce hallucination.
Monitoring and verifying agent behavior during execution to ensure correctness and safety.
Training technique that filters generated samples by quality before fine-tuning on accepted outputs.
Training approach where model distills knowledge from grouped relative comparisons of its own outputs.
Enforcing output structure through schema definitions (JSON Schema, TypeScript types, etc.).
Flexible parsing strategies that tolerate minor formatting errors in LLM outputs.
Providing validation error messages back to models for iterative output correction.
Tracing type definitions to their atomic components for complete validation.
Structural Similarity Index Measure used for comparing visual elements in UI testing.
Verbal self-reflection technique enabling agents to learn from failed attempts.
Interleaving reasoning traces and task-specific actions in LLM prompting.
Systematic exploration of action spaces using tree-structured search algorithms.
Agent-computer interface system designed for autonomous software engineering.
Open-source platform for building AI-powered software development agents.
